files.description: System architecture with state diagrams, protocol flow, rate-limit handling strategy, and component breakdown. Essential context for understanding service boundaries and the spawn-to-completion lifecycle.

Project conventions: singleton pattern, in-place mutation rules, async race prevention patterns, three-tier error handling, state machine transitions, token rotation flow, kill escalation protocol, template system, and critical gotchas. Read before reviewing any PR.
Core type definitions: TaskState, TaskStatus enum, shared interfaces, error classification constants (ROTATABLE_STATUS_CODES). Defines the data model that all services share.
Model ID definitions, ENABLE_OPUS visibility control, resolveModel() logic including the planner-always-Opus override. Review when model selection or role dispatch changes.

Zod v4 schemas for all tool inputs. Shared field schemas (timeout, model, depends_on, labels, context files) and per-role schemas. The source of truth for input validation — must match tool inputSchema definitions.
Timeout constants and environment variable reading. Defines task timeout, stall warning, TTL, and broken pipe exit thresholds.
User-facing tool reference, resource URI specs, agent template descriptions, workflows, and troubleshooting. Useful for verifying that code changes align with documented behavior.

Code review guidelines: critical areas (STDIO integrity, state machine, token handling, process lifecycle, session binding), security checklist, conventions, performance patterns, and anti-patterns with good/bad code examples.
